ccel.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

title: Concerning Conception and Articulation.

source.title: NPNF2-09. Hilary of Poitiers, John of Damascus

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Again the reasoning part of the soul is divided into conception and articulation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Conception is an activity of the soul originating in the reason without resulting in utterance.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: Accordingly, often, even when we are silent we run through a whole speech in our minds, and hold discussions in our dreams.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: And it is this faculty chiefly which constitutes us all reasoning beings.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: For those who are dumb by birth or have lost their voice through some disease or injury, are just as much reasoning beings.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: But articulation by voice or in the different dialects requires energy: that is to say, the word is articulated by the tongue and mouth, and this is why it is named articulation.

verses.book: An Exact Exposition of the Orthodox Faith.
verses.text: It is, indeed, the messenger of thought, and it is because of it that we are called speaking beings.
